_review.note: Machine-parsed from the official rating sheet. plan.md F3: nobody may be graded against this until a human confirms it.
_review.paraphrase_note: Descriptions and level descriptors are restated in our own words (plan.md §20). Criterion names and every point value are exactly as published on the official rating sheet.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No programming language was chosen or demonstrated.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: A programming language was shown, but no reasoning for its selection was provided.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The chosen language was presented, but some aspects of the selection process were not fully elaborated.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The programming language was presented, accompanied by a thorough explanation of how it was chosen, incorporating relevant industry terms.

criteria.name: Appropriate use of comments, naming conventions, and proper formatting
criteria.description: The proper application of code comments, naming standards, and formatting.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No explanatory notes were included in the code.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Comments were present, but they did not make logical sense or were unhelpful.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The comments included in the code effectively aided the judges in understanding the demonstration.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The code contains comments that are sensible, helpful, and comprehensive.

criteria.name: The program is modular in a way that makes logical, readable sense
criteria.description: The program's structure is modular, allowing for clear and understandable organization.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: The program's structure is neither logical nor easy to read.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: The program's organization is either logical or readable, but not both simultaneously.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The program is structured in a way that is both logical and easy to comprehend.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The program demonstrates an expert level of programming understanding.

criteria.name: UX Design: User Journey, Design Rationale, Accessibility Features
criteria.description: User Experience (UX) Design, encompassing the user's journey, the reasoning behind design choices, and integrated accessibility features.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No user experience design was shown.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Only a few components of the user experience design were presented.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The user experience design was presented, but lacked certain specifics.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The user experience design was presented, with thorough explanations of the design's underlying reasons, the typical user path, and specific accessibility features.

criteria.name: User interface is intuitive or clear instructions are provided
criteria.description: The user interface is either easy to understand without guidance or comes with explicit directions.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No instructions were provided, and the interface is not intuitive.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Instructions were given, but they were not intuitive.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Suitable and clear instructions are provided.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The program is intuitive, and clear instructions are also provided.

criteria.name: Users can easily navigate between pages
criteria.description: Users can effortlessly move from one page or section to another.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No help menu or system for moving between sections was included.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: A basic help menu and functional navigation are included.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The interface contains no spelling errors, features an interactive help menu, and has no navigation issues.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The program also incorporates an advanced feature, such as an interactive Q&A system.

criteria.name: User input is validated
criteria.description: User-provided data is checked for correctness.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: User-provided data is not checked for validity.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: An attempt was made to validate user input, but it failed to capture important unusual cases or was performed incorrectly.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: User-provided data is properly checked for validity.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Input validation is applied at both the structural (syntactical) and meaning (semantic) levels.

criteria.name: Program addresses all parts of the prompt
criteria.description: The program fully responds to every component of the given task or problem statement.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: The program does not address the assigned topic or problem.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: The program addresses the assigned topic or problem to a minimal extent.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The program completely addresses the assigned topic or problem.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The program completely addresses the assigned topic or problem, and the relationship is clarified within the instructions.

criteria.name: Program generates a presentable report
criteria.description: The program produces a report that is suitable for presentation.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: Output reports are either inaccurate or unavailable.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Output reports are insufficient for data analysis.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Output reports are free of errors and provide all necessary information for data analysis.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Output reports enable the user to customize and analyze information.

criteria.name: Data storage
criteria.description: The method used for storing data.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: No data is stored.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Variables are used inconsistently, or in a manner that is unclear.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Variables and their usage are clear. Variables store data that is appropriately discarded when no longer needed. Each variable serves a single purpose, and the correct data type is employed.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: More complex data storage methods, such as arrays and lists, are utilized where appropriate, and variable scope is logical.

criteria.name: Statements are well-organized and clearly stated
criteria.description: The presentation's statements are structured effectively and articulated with clarity.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: The competitor(s) did not appear ready.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: The competitor(s) were prepared, but the presentation's progression lacked logical order.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: The presentation proceeded in a logical sequence.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: The presentation flowed in a logical sequence, and the statements were very well organized.

criteria.name: Consistently displays confidence, poised body language, engaging eye contact, and effective voice projection
criteria.description: Consistently exhibits self-assurance, composed posture, engaging eye contact, and effective vocal projection.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: Did not show any of the specified abilities.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Demonstrated 1-2 of the listed abilities (self-assurance, body language, eye contact, or vocal projection).

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Demonstrated 3-4 of the listed abilities (self-assurance, body language, eye contact, or vocal projection).

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Demonstrated all abilities, thereby improving the overall presentation.

criteria.name: Demonstrates the ability to effectively answer questions
criteria.description: Shows the capacity to answer questions competently.

levels.label: Not Demonstrated
levels.descriptor: Does not respond to questions, or responses are entirely irrelevant.

levels.label: Below Expectations
levels.descriptor: Provides incomplete or unclear answers and shows limited comprehension.

levels.label: Meets Expectations
levels.descriptor: Responds accurately and clearly to most questions, indicating adequate understanding.

levels.label: Exceeds Expectations
levels.descriptor: Responds with confidence, accuracy, and clarity, offering insightful answers that enhance the overall presentation.

criteria.name: Adherence to Competitive Events Guidelines
criteria.description: Execution Aligned with Guidelines: (All criteria must be met) Used only allowable technology devices (sizing specs followed; no projectors) Presentation aligned with the assigned topic Maintained professional boundaries during set-up time (no interaction with judges) Did leave materials behind after the presentation Links & QR codes were not used (not actively scanned or scanned by judges) Audio was presented without external speakers (preliminary round) Avoided use of food or live animals

levels.label: Not Followed
levels.descriptor: The competitor(s) did not adhere to the guidelines.

levels.label: Followed
levels.descriptor: Execution Aligned with Guidelines: (All criteria must be met) Used only allowable technology devices (sizing specs followed; no projectors) Presentation aligned with the assigned topic Maintained professional boundaries during set-up time (no interaction with judges) Did leave materials behind after the presentation Links & QR codes were not used (not actively scanned or scanned by judges) Audio was presented without external speakers (preliminary round) Avoided use of food or live animals
